context.description: A philosophical discussion examines the limits of analogies between waking and dreaming as a way to reason about a hypothetical consciousness above ordinary waking life. It introduces the section "Unmögliche Blicke" and turns toward additional evidence such as psychoactive substances.
normalization_notes: Book title inferred from source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: The text argues that analogies between waking and dreaming cannot fully explain a hypothetical state of consciousness above waking life. It emphasizes the limits of human knowledge, then opens the section "Unmögliche Blicke" and points toward psychoactive substances as a further clue.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page on the limits of analogies between waking and dreaming, consciousness above waking life, and psychoactive substances. Section: Unmögliche Blicke.
